issues: COLD-TURN BRIDGE RACE: cursor's FIRST bridge tool call failed — the clean stream literally says 'Retrying with the shell tool after the bridge connection failed.' It fell back to its native Shell, then re-ran GetMcpTools and the bridge worked (CallMcpTool x3 wrote/read report.md). This is the same cold-turn MCP-readiness race the claude adapter's WithMCPReadyFile gate fixes — that gate is NOT yet wired into the cursor adapter, so cursor's opening bridge call can race the connection. Actionable: extend the readiness gate (MCP_READY_FILE) to cursor/codex/pi.
cursor reaches the bridge via GetMcpTools/CallMcpTool meta-tools and also uses its native Shell; the run is bridge + shell, not bridge-only.
agent_review.notes: Real-bridge path exercised end to end: after the initial retry, cursor's CallMcpTool went mcpbridge → executor → real execute_shell_command to write report.md, which on disk is a correct markdown table with the build id BUILD_ID_300b30d7ba09 (only ever in the file). The clean stream (Source != terminal) is coherent narration interleaved with the tools plus the final markdown table verbatim — a no-terminal UI gets the renderable table, no ANSI. Signed good; the cold-turn race above is the tracked follow-up.
